straight.name: Urea
straight.rate: Varies by crop – 100–250 kg/ha for most field crops
straight.precautions: Avoid application on standing water, apply in cool hours to reduce volatilization loss, do not mix with other fertilizers directly

straight.name: DAP (Di-Ammonium Phosphate)
straight.application: Basal application – mix in soil before sowing
straight.precautions: Do not apply with urea simultaneously. Good for acidic to neutral soils.

straight.name: SSP (Single Super Phosphate)
straight.application: Basal application; ideal for oilseeds and pulses (sulphur benefit)
straight.precautions: Store in dry place. Good for alkaline soils.

straight.name: MOP (Muriate of Potash)
straight.application: Basal or top dressing. Avoid on saline soils.
straight.precautions: Avoid on chloride-sensitive crops (potato, tobacco, grapes). Use SOP instead.

straight.name: Ammonium Sulphate
straight.application: Top dressing; ideal for sulphur-deficient soils
straight.precautions: Acidifying effect – good for alkaline soils.

complex.name: NPK 10:26:26
complex.use: Basal dose for vegetables, fruits, oilseeds

complex.name: NPK 12:32:16
complex.use: High phosphorus crops; transplanted rice, sugarcane

complex.name: NPK 20:20:0:13
complex.use: Oilseeds, pulses, cereals on sulphur-deficient soils

organic.name: FYM (Farm Yard Manure)
organic.benefits: Improves soil structure, water retention, biological activity
organic.preparation: Compost cattle/buffalo dung mixed with urine-soaked bedding for 3–4 months

organic.name: Vermicompost
organic.benefits: Enhanced nutrient availability, improves microbial activity, plant growth promoter
organic.production: Use earthworms (Eisenia fetida) in vermibeds. Ready in 45–60 days.

organic.name: Neem Cake
organic.benefits: Fertilizer + nematicidal + insecticidal properties. Inhibits nitrification – slow release N.

organic.name: Green Manure
organic.benefit: Adds 60–90 kg N/ha when incorporated at 45–60 days. Improves soil organic matter.

biofertilizers.name: Rhizobium
biofertilizers.benefit: Fixes 100–200 kg N/ha through symbiosis. Apply as seed treatment.
biofertilizers.application: Mix 200 g Rhizobium culture with 300 ml water + jaggery solution. Coat seeds, dry in shade, sow within 24 hours.

biofertilizers.name: Azotobacter
biofertilizers.benefit: Free-living N fixation (20–25 kg N/ha). Also produces growth-promoting substances.
biofertilizers.application: Seed treatment or soil application at 4–5 kg culture/ha

biofertilizers.name: PSB (Phosphate Solubilizing Bacteria)
biofertilizers.benefit: Solubilizes fixed phosphorus in soil, making it available to plants. Saves 25% P fertilizer.
biofertilizers.application: Seed treatment or soil application at 5 kg culture/ha

biofertilizers.name: Blue-Green Algae (BGA) / Azolla
biofertilizers.benefit: Fixes 25–30 kg N/ha. Azolla grows in rice fields and can be incorporated as green manure.
biofertilizers.application: Broadcast 1 kg fresh Azolla/10 m2 or use BGA culture

general_principles: Always base fertilizer application on soil test results
Apply phosphorus and potassium as basal dose (pre-sowing/transplanting)
Split nitrogen application reduces loss and improves efficiency
Micronutrients should be applied only when deficiency is confirmed by soil test
Combine chemical fertilizers with organic manures for best results

spray_guidelines.foliar_nutrition: Apply during cool hours (early morning or late evening). Avoid spraying in rain or strong sun.
spray_guidelines.compatibility: Check fertilizer compatibility before mixing. Most DAP-Urea combinations are safe in spray solutions.
spray_guidelines.frequency: Foliar sprays are supplementary to soil application, not a replacement.
